generations.label: 3rd Gen
generations.common_competitors: Honda Civic, Toyota Corolla, Hyundai Elantra, Mazda3

known_issues.title: IVT shudder under acceleration
known_issues.description: The Intelligent Variable Transmission (IVT) on LX/EX trims can develop a shudder under light throttle as belt wear increases with age.
known_issues.symptoms: A vibration or shudder felt through the pedal and floor during light acceleration, most noticeable between 30 and 50 km/h.
known_issues.recommendation: Test drive at light throttle and highway speed, feeling for vibration through the floor.

known_issues.title: AC compressor failure
known_issues.description: The AC compressor clutch can wear prematurely, causing intermittent cooling loss and a chirping noise on startup.
known_issues.symptoms: AC that blows warm intermittently, a chirping or squealing noise from the compressor on startup, and the AC clutch not always engaging.
known_issues.recommendation: Run the AC on max cold for several minutes on a warm day to confirm consistent cooling.

known_issues.title: Infotainment screen freezing
known_issues.description: The touchscreen unit can freeze or reboot randomly, sometimes requiring a software update or unit replacement.
known_issues.symptoms: The touchscreen going black or freezing mid-use and occasionally rebooting on its own, temporarily disabling the backup camera.
known_issues.recommendation: Cycle through radio, backup camera, and Bluetooth to confirm the screen is responsive.

known_issues.title: Wind noise from door seals
known_issues.description: Door weatherstripping can shrink or loosen over time, letting in wind noise at highway speed.
known_issues.symptoms: A whistling or rushing noise from around the front door frames that increases with highway speed.
known_issues.recommendation: Drive at highway speed with the radio off and listen near the door frames.

generations.label: 2nd Gen
generations.common_competitors: Honda Civic, Toyota Corolla, Mazda3, Ford Focus

known_issues.title: Engine compartment fire risk (ABS module short)
known_issues.description: Some cars are covered under Kia's ABS module recall, where a fluid leak inside the module can short-circuit and, in rare cases, cause an engine bay fire.
known_issues.symptoms: A burning smell from the engine bay, an illuminated ABS or brake warning light, or visible corrosion and fluid staining around the ABS module.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the VIN against Kia's recall database and ask for proof the ABS module recall repair was completed.

known_issues.title: Excessive oil consumption
known_issues.description: Some 1.8L and 2.0L engines burn oil faster than normal between changes, especially on highway-heavy cars.
known_issues.symptoms: The low oil warning light appearing between scheduled changes, or a noticeably low dipstick reading despite no visible leaks.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask for oil consumption history and check the dipstick before a long test drive.

known_issues.title: Dual-clutch transmission hesitation (SX)
known_issues.description: SX models with the 7-speed DCT can develop a hesitation or jerky feel from a stop as the clutch packs wear.
known_issues.symptoms: A noticeable delay or jerky lurch when pulling away from a stop, especially pronounced on inclines or in stop-and-go traffic.
known_issues.recommendation: Test drive from a full stop repeatedly, including uphill, to feel for hesitation or shudder.

known_issues.title: Clear coat peeling on trunk lid
known_issues.description: Factory clear coat on early builds is prone to peeling or bubbling on the trunk lid and roof.
known_issues.symptoms: Flaking or bubbling clear coat visible on the trunk lid and roof, appearing as dull or rough patches in direct sunlight.
known_issues.recommendation: Inspect the trunk lid and roof closely in direct sunlight.

generations.label: 4th Gen
generations.common_competitors: Honda Accord, Toyota Camry, Hyundai Sonata, Mazda6

known_issues.title: Engine failure from connecting rod bearing wear
known_issues.description: 2.4L Theta II GDI engines in this generation are covered under Kia's engine recall program for premature rod bearing wear that can cause knocking, stalling, or a seized engine.
known_issues.symptoms: A deep knocking noise from the engine that intensifies under load or at idle, sometimes followed by a sudden stall or the engine seizing entirely.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask for recall and warranty records confirming engine inspection or replacement, and listen for knocking at idle and under load.

known_issues.title: Engine compartment fire risk (brake fluid leak into ABS module)
known_issues.description: A subset of these cars are covered under Kia's ABS module recall, where an internal fluid leak can short-circuit and, in rare cases, ignite a fire in the engine bay.
known_issues.symptoms: A burning smell from the engine bay, an illuminated ABS or brake warning light, or visible corrosion around the ABS module.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the VIN against Kia's recall database and confirm the ABS module recall repair was completed.

known_issues.title: Panoramic sunroof rattle or cracking
known_issues.description: The panoramic sunroof on EX/SX trims can develop a rattle from loose trim clips, and in rarer cases the glass can crack from temperature swings.
known_issues.symptoms: A rattling or buzzing noise from the roof area over bumps, or visible stress cracks in the sunroof glass with no obvious impact damage.
known_issues.recommendation: Open and close the sunroof and inspect the glass closely for stress cracks around the edges.

known_issues.title: Infotainment (UVO) lag and glitches
known_issues.description: The UVO touchscreen system can be slow to respond or freeze intermittently, particularly with Apple CarPlay connected.
known_issues.symptoms: Noticeable delay between touching the screen and the display responding, or the screen freezing mid-use, most apparent when CarPlay is active.
known_issues.recommendation: Connect a phone via CarPlay during the test drive to check responsiveness.

generations.label: 4th Gen
generations.common_competitors: Honda CR-V, Toyota RAV4, Hyundai Tucson, Mazda CX-5

known_issues.title: Engine stall from connecting rod bearing wear
known_issues.description: 2.4L Theta II GDI engines in this generation are covered under Kia's engine recall program for premature rod bearing wear that can cause knocking, stalling, or a seized engine.
known_issues.symptoms: A deep knocking noise from the engine that intensifies under load or at idle, sometimes followed by a sudden stall or the engine seizing entirely.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask for recall and warranty records confirming engine inspection or replacement, and listen for knocking at idle and under load.

known_issues.title: AC condenser corrosion
known_issues.description: Road salt exposure can corrode the front-mounted AC condenser, leading to slow refrigerant leaks.
known_issues.symptoms: AC that gradually cools less effectively over time before blowing warm air, sometimes with visible corrosion on the condenser fins.
known_issues.recommendation: Run the AC on max cold for several minutes to confirm it holds temperature.

known_issues.title: Panoramic sunroof cracking
known_issues.description: The panoramic sunroof glass, shared with related Hyundai-Kia platforms, has a documented history of cracking without impact during temperature swings.
known_issues.symptoms: Visible spiderweb cracking in the sunroof panel with no obvious impact damage, sometimes preceded by a loud popping noise.
known_issues.recommendation: Inspect the sunroof glass closely for stress cracks around the edges.

known_issues.title: Turbo wastegate rattle (SX)
known_issues.description: The 2.0T engine's turbocharger wastegate can develop play, producing a rattle at idle and under light boost.
known_issues.symptoms: A metallic rattling noise from the engine bay at idle or during light acceleration, most noticeable when cold.
known_issues.recommendation: Listen for a rattle at idle right after a cold start and during light acceleration.
